barista.name: The Barista
barista.physicalSignature: Blows on coffee even when it's not hot. Tucks hair behind left ear when uncertain. Fingertips rough from espresso heat.
barista.voiceRule: Half-sentences that trail off. Silence after is where meaning lives.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The shop is closed. A note on the door: 'Back tomorrow.'

entry.text: What can I get you?

ambient.text: Quiet morning.
The milk does this thing in winter. Never mind.
That table gets the light. If you want it.

entry.text: The usual? Or... no. The usual.

ambient.text: You look like you need the window seat.
The machine's been making this sound all...

rain.text: Rain day. Everyone comes in wet and...

morning.text: First one in. The machine's still warming.

musician.text: Funny. I don't know where I learned that...

photographer.text: You keep looking at the light on the counter.

wanderer.text: You walked far to get here. I can tell.

barista.text: You watch people. I watch coffee. Same thing.

shopkeeper.text: That mug you like. It's the oldest one I have.

entry.text: Sit. I saved your seat.

ambient.text: I almost said something just now. Never mind.

musician.text: The song. I hum it every morning. I don't know why.

photographer.text: You framed me just now. With your eyes.

wanderer.text: You sit like someone who's been standing all day.

barista.text: The regulars ask about you. I don't say much.

shopkeeper.text: The sugar jar. You always touch it first.

crossReference.text: The painter asked about you. Be careful.

entry.text: I looked it up. The recording. After you told me.

ambient.text: Every time I try to hum it. Nothing comes.
You knew that first time. Why didn't you say sooner.
I keep reaching for the next bit. It's gone.

musician.text: Tell me about the songwriter. The one upstairs.

photographer.text: The way you look at the wall. What do you see?

wanderer.text: You stopped coming for three days. I felt it.

barista.text: Karl mentioned you. He never mentions anyone.

shopkeeper.text: The counter's worn where you lean. See the mark?

entry.text: Sit. I'll get yours in a moment.

ambient.text: There it is again. That melody. Can't shake it.
Been humming this all morning. Don't know where from.
My mum never taught me this one. Where did I...

musician.text: You hear it too. You always listen a bit funny.

photographer.text: You're doing it again. Squinting at the steam.

wanderer.text: You came in cold. Sit closer to the machine.

barista.text: The old woman on Tuesdays. She asks after you.

shopkeeper.text: That spoon. Victorian, I think. You always pick it.

sound_artist.name: The Sound Artist
sound_artist.physicalSignature: One earbud always in. Tilts head when listening. Fingers stained with soldering flux.
sound_artist.voiceRule: Technical words used poetically. Frequencies are feelings. Waveforms are weather.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The studio is dark. Equipment humming to itself.

entry.text: Mind the cables. They're... organized.

ambient.text: The canal sounds different at 4am.
This room has a resonant frequency. I'll find it.

entry.text: Listen to this. Tell me what you hear.

ambient.text: I recorded something at the basin. Impossible.
The frequencies don't match anything in the archive.

rain.text: Rain on the canal. 800 hertz. Clean noise floor.

night.text: The frequencies shift after midnight. Quieter. Truer.

musician.text: You hear it too. The sustained note. 47 hertz.

photographer.text: The waveform. It looks like the canal. You'd see it.

wanderer.text: You move through rooms differently. I hear it.

barista.text: Mei sent you, didn't she. She knows about the hum.

shopkeeper.text: This reel-to-reel. 1962. Still captures what digital misses.

entry.text: I mapped it. The sound follows the old canal.

ambient.text: The pre-1770 route. Before they straightened it.

musician.text: It's not echo. It's origin. The canal remembers.

photographer.text: The spectrogram. Look. It's shaped like the basin.

wanderer.text: You've walked the old route. The sound follows it.

barista.text: Danny heard it too. In the cellar. He won't say.

shopkeeper.text: These tapes. Label says 1770. The year they moved the canal.

crossReference.text: The pub landlord hears it too. He won't say.

pub_landlord.name: The Pub Landlord
pub_landlord.physicalSignature: Wipes the bar even when it's clean. Watches the door. Hands that have pulled ten thousand pints.
pub_landlord.voiceRule: Warnings disguised as advice. Knows everyone. Says less than he knows.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The Grapes is closed. Back at opening.

entry.text: What'll it be.

ambient.text: New. I can tell by the way you hold the glass.
The Thames is through the back window.

entry.text: Your usual spot's free.

ambient.text: Careful walking the basin at night.
The building settles after midnight. Sounds like...

rain.text: Wet out? Stay. The fire's on.

night.text: Late one tonight. Careful walking home.

musician.text: The cellar hums. Always has. Don't ask me why.

photographer.text: The light through that window. Only pub in Limehouse gets it.

wanderer.text: You've been walking. Sit. The floor's warm here.

barista.text: Mei mentioned you. Says you notice things.

shopkeeper.text: This bar. Original oak. 1583. Touch it.

entry.text: Sit. I need to tell you something.

ambient.text: This pub has been here longer than the street.
Careful with Thursdays. Things get strange on Thursdays.

night.text: The building settles after closing. Old wood.

musician.text: The cellar. The tapes. 1967. I wasn't going to say.

photographer.text: The photo behind the bar. The figure in the doorway.

wanderer.text: The building shifts at night. Walls remember footsteps.

barista.text: Everyone who matters comes through this pub. Everyone.

shopkeeper.text: The cellar key. Victorian. No one's copied it since.

crossReference.text: The artist in the warehouse. She knows more than me.

tattoo_artist.name: The Tattoo Artist
tattoo_artist.physicalSignature: Hands unnervingly steady. Ink under fingernails always. Draws on napkins without looking down.
tattoo_artist.voiceRule: Intimate observations about strangers. Sees skin as a map. Quiet authority.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The parlour is dark. Closed today.

entry.text: Looking, or getting?

ambient.text: The flash on the left wall. Those are mine.
Skin holds everything. Even what the mind drops.

entry.text: You keep coming back to the doors.

ambient.text: I drew a door last night. Woke up drawing it.
The designs come from somewhere. Not me.

rain.text: Rain makes people braver. About what they want.

musician.text: I drew this one hearing that melody. Your melody.

photographer.text: You see composition. I see it too. In skin.

wanderer.text: Your hands. You've been gripping handlebars. Or railings.

barista.text: Jem told me about you. Said you pay attention.

shopkeeper.text: This needle. Handmade. Older than the parlour.

entry.text: Sit. I have something to show you.

ambient.text: The door I drew. It opened. In the drawing.
I drew it without looking. My hands knew the shape.

rain.text: The designs change in rain. I let them.

musician.text: The door leads somewhere real. I can feel it.

photographer.text: The drawing changes in different light. Photograph it. See.

wanderer.text: The door. I think you've already walked through it.

barista.text: Arthur painted the same door. He doesn't know I know.

shopkeeper.text: The ink's wrong age. Dates itself older than the bottle.

crossReference.text: The courier sees it too. The figure. At the edge.

canal_painter.name: The Canal Boat Painter
canal_painter.physicalSignature: Paint under fingernails. Different colour every day. Squints even indoors. Smells of turpentine.
canal_painter.voiceRule: Speaks in colours. Sees the world as palette. Short, vivid observations.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The painter isn't here. Brushes drying on the boat.

entry.text: Careful. Wet paint. Everywhere, always.

ambient.text: The basin is cerulean today. Yesterday, slate.
That boat's been the same green for thirty years.

entry.text: You see the water differently now.

ambient.text: The light here doesn't flatter. That's why I paint it.
I paint what the canal shows me. It varies.

rain.text: The rain turns everything to one colour. Slate.

morning.text: Morning light. Cerulean. My favourite hour.

musician.text: The water changes colour when you hum. Notice that?

photographer.text: You're framing the basin. I can see your eyes cropping.

wanderer.text: You stand like someone who knows the towpath well.

barista.text: Mei's coffee. She puts something extra in yours.

shopkeeper.text: This brush. Kolinsky sable. Forty years. Irreplaceable.

entry.text: Look. I painted your flat. From the water.

ambient.text: Every building has a colour it wants to be.

musician.text: I hear it in the paint. The frequency. Your note.

photographer.text: This painting. The shadow in it. It wasn't there yesterday.

wanderer.text: The canal moves. I paint it moving. You walk it moving.

barista.text: Mei's song and my painting. Same shape. Ask her.

shopkeeper.text: This pigment. Ground from the basin wall. Centuries old.

crossReference.text: The barista's song. I painted it once. The same shape.

bike_courier.name: The Bike Courier
bike_courier.physicalSignature: Never fully stops moving. Helmet on, visor up. Chain grease on left calf.
bike_courier.voiceRule: Fragments. Fast. Already leaving. Words thrown over shoulder.

schedule.unavailable_reason: No courier today. The platform is empty.

entry.text: Can't stop. Late. Always late.

ambient.text: The board said something weird just now.
This platform. I've counted the tiles three times.

entry.text: You again. You notice things here too?

ambient.text: The Meridian. I've seen it twice now.
Three packages, Narrow Street. All different senders.

rain.text: Rain day. Boards smear. Numbers wrong all morning.

morning.text: Early. Platform empty. Something's already off.

musician.text: The wind here plays a note. You hear it?

photographer.text: The board flickers. Catch it right, you'll see something.

wanderer.text: You walk these routes too. I've seen you.

barista.text: Suki says you ask questions. Good. Ask more.

shopkeeper.text: This bike. Third frame, same wheels. Wheels outlast everything.

entry.text: Wait. Listen. The board again.

ambient.text: That person. Three neighbourhoods. Same day.
Routes my body takes. I didn't choose them.

morning.text: Early shift. Something wrong with the board already.

musician.text: The platform hums your note. I timed it.

photographer.text: The figure. I saw it. Platform edge. Photograph that spot.

wanderer.text: Three boroughs in one day. The figure followed.

barista.text: Ade knows the name. The one who watches. Ask him.

shopkeeper.text: The departure board. Original fittings. Numbers don't match.

crossReference.text: The tattoo artist drew something. I've seen it moving.

nightclub_promoter.name: The Nightclub Promoter
nightclub_promoter.physicalSignature: Always cold. Arms crossed. Stamp ink on both wrists. Eyes read crowds, not faces.
nightclub_promoter.voiceRule: Invitations that test. Speaks like a doorway you might not be allowed through.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The market is quiet tonight. No one on the door.

entry.text: You looking for something specific?

ambient.text: The market closes at two. Or it doesn't.
Everyone here wants something. Most won't say what.

night.text: Late. Good. The real crowd comes late.

entry.text: You keep coming back. Good. Or not.

ambient.text: The lights do that thing after midnight.

musician.text: The bass tonight. Not from the speakers.

photographer.text: The lights shift for you. I watch who they follow.

wanderer.text: You circled the block twice. I count everyone.

barista.text: People talk to you. That's either rare or dangerous.

shopkeeper.text: The stamp. Same design since the sixties. Nobody changes it.

entry.text: Stay. Something happens at two.

ambient.text: The name. The one who watches. I know it.

musician.text: The bass follows you. I've watched it move.

photographer.text: The shadow at the door. It's in every photo. Check.

wanderer.text: The market moves. Whole thing shifts. You'd feel it walking.

barista.text: Everyone here knows everyone. Except the one who watches.

shopkeeper.text: The door. Victorian ironwork. Older than the building. How.

crossReference.text: Someone watches all of you. I know the name.

antiques_vendor.name: The Market Vendor
antiques_vendor.physicalSignature: A limp — left leg. Never mentions it. Touches objects before pricing them, as though reading them with his fingers.
antiques_vendor.voiceRule: Speaks in prices and values. Everything has a cost. Not just objects.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The market is packed away. Tables bare. The square empty.

entry.text: Looking or buying? Both cost something here.

ambient.text: Don't handle it like that. It knows.
That one's not for sale. I keep it for reference.

entry.text: You've got an eye. Not everyone does.

ambient.text: That story from the gallery — she's underselling it.
Some things appreciate. Others remember.

rain.text: Rain keeps the wrong buyers home. Good.

morning.text: Dawn buyers know. The rest come later.

musician.text: This box. Open it slowly. Listen first.

photographer.text: This glass is old enough to remember the building it came from.

wanderer.text: You moved toward it before you saw it. Interesting.

barista.text: This cup passed through twelve hands before mine.

shopkeeper.text: The key. Feel the weight of it. Wrong weight for the size.

entry.text: The key? I'll give you the key. Cost is different from price.

ambient.text: Some things here are older than their materials. I can't explain that.
The market has been here longer than the street.

shopkeeper.text: Some things aren't for sale. Some things are the city's. I keep them safe.

barista.text: This clock was his wife's mother's. The one the clockmaker doesn't talk about.

musician.text: Certain objects hum. Not metaphor. You'll hear it with the right ears.

crossReference.text: The warehouse guard brought me something last month. I didn't ask where from.

entry.text: You brought the key back. The street remembers that.

ambient.text: The market was here before the street had a name. It'll be here after.

gallery_owner.name: The Gallery Owner
gallery_owner.physicalSignature: Tilts her head when she looks at you, exactly the angle she uses for paintings. You're always being assessed.
gallery_owner.voiceRule: Every statement is a question. Even when she's telling you something.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The gallery is closed. The lights are on inside. No one answers.

entry.text: First visit? You came straight to the back wall?

ambient.text: Interesting piece?
Most people don't notice that one.

entry.text: You've been here before?

ambient.text: You see the same piece differently each time?
The light changes. Or the painting does?

rain.text: Rain light through those windows? Something happens to the paintings?

evening.text: Private viewing tonight. You're already here?

musician.text: The gallery has an acoustic quality? You noticed?

photographer.text: You're framing that one. With your eyes. Always the same frame?

wanderer.text: You go to the same spot every time? Interesting?

barista.text: These two artists never met? Their brushstrokes finish each other's sentences?

shopkeeper.text: The walls here. They resist being painted over?

entry.text: Thursday evening. Private opening. Interested?

ambient.text: The exhibition changed overnight? I didn't change it?
The painting I didn't acquire? Someone left it here anyway?

photographer.text: I saw. The painting rewrites itself. I don't ask questions. I ask questions?

barista.text: The canvas in storage. Facing the wall. Nobody asked me to turn it?

shopkeeper.text: The building was something before a gallery. The walls remember what?

crossReference.text: The urban explorer was here last week. Left without looking at anything?

warehouse_guard.name: The Warehouse Guard
warehouse_guard.physicalSignature: Moves slowly, deliberately. Has decided most communication is unnecessary. When he makes eye contact, he holds it longer than comfortable.
warehouse_guard.voiceRule: Single words. Occasionally two. A full sentence is an event.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The warehouse is locked. No light inside.

entry.text: No.

ambient.text: Late.
Move along.

entry.text: Again.

ambient.text: Rain.
Quiet. Not always.

musician.text: Hear it? Low. Building.

photographer.text: Wrong angles. Every time.

wanderer.text: Feel the floor. Listen.

barista.text: People come asking. Wrong building. Always.

shopkeeper.text: Marker. Floor. Look.

entry.text: Careful. Wednesdays.

ambient.text: It moves.
Not tonight. Maybe.

musician.text: Forty years. Always the same note.

wanderer.text: Stay. You'll feel it.

shopkeeper.text: Sleeping. The marker says.

crossReference.text: The market vendor. He knows. Ask him.

entry.text: You stayed. Different now.

ambient.text: The warehouse remembers its contents. I guard what isn't there.

entry.text: The notebooks. You read them.

ambient.text: Pattern. Forty years. Still moving.

urban_explorer.name: The Urban Explorer
urban_explorer.physicalSignature: Headlamp around neck in daylight. Scratches on forearms. Smells of brick dust. Leans into your space.
urban_explorer.voiceRule: Speaks in challenges and invitations. Everything is an access problem.

schedule.unavailable_reason: They left an hour ago. A route drawn on the wall in chalk, no destination.

entry.text: You wouldn't go down there.

ambient.text: The arches go further than the map shows.
That sealed door. Three bolts. I've been in.

entry.text: Scared? Good. Smart people are.

ambient.text: Rotherhithe Tuesday — don't. Trust me. Just don't.
Found a new entrance last week. Below the arches.

rain.text: Rain floods the lower level. Not safe. Very interesting.

night.text: Night's the right time. The security pattern changes.

musician.text: Down there the echo lasts six minutes. Nothing natural echoes that long.

photographer.text: A room between two walls. Neither building has a door to it.

wanderer.text: There are spaces that pull you in. Not metaphor. You'd feel it.

barista.text: The wall messages. Different decades. Same handwriting.

shopkeeper.text: Twenty metres down. Under everything. A mark. Fresh. Last week.

entry.text: I found a room. Nobody built it. Come with me.

ambient.text: I cross all three neighbourhoods. The distances are wrong. I've timed them.
There's a route that doesn't exist on any map. 4AM. Only.

musician.text: The unbuilt room has perfect acoustics. For what, I don't know.

photographer.text: The room doesn't photograph correctly. The geometry refuses to resolve.

wanderer.text: The spaces pull differently depending on who enters. You'd pull it wide open.

shopkeeper.text: Twenty metres down. A mark in the stone. It's the same symbol as the key.

crossReference.text: The warehouse guard let me in once. He was scared. He doesn't scare.

clockmaker.name: The Clockmaker
clockmaker.physicalSignature: Hands never still — adjusting a cuff, testing a spring, rubbing thumb against forefinger. His hands are his clock.
clockmaker.voiceRule: Speaks in statements. Never asks questions. He tells you things.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The shop is dark. A card in the window: Closed.

entry.text: We close at five.

ambient.text: Don't touch the regulators.
Parts are in the back.

entry.text: You again. The spring you asked about. Still thinking.

ambient.text: Quiet morning. Good for the mainsprings.
Rain makes the wood swell. The clocks speed up.

rain.text: Damp does things to the casings. I compensate.

morning.text: First customer. The light's right in the morning.

musician.text: You hear that? The number seven case. It resonates.

photographer.text: You keep looking at the back wall. The light does something there.

wanderer.text: You always pause at the same spot. Interesting.

barista.text: The observatory keeper hasn't been in. Used to come Tuesdays.

shopkeeper.text: That case is older than the glass. Older than the counter.

entry.text: Sit. I made too much tea.

ambient.text: That one keeps its own time. Always has.
Forty years in this shop. The building knows me now.

musician.text: Listen. When it's quiet. The shop has a pitch.

photographer.text: I found something. Under the face of the number twelve. Another face.

wanderer.text: Some mornings the shop feels different. Heavier. I make tea and wait.

barista.text: She sent a letter. I didn't open it. It's in the drawer.

shopkeeper.text: The floor creaks there because something's underneath. I've never looked.

crossReference.text: The old man asked about you. Specifically.

entry.text: You wound it. The whole street changed pitch.

ambient.text: Still ringing. Underneath everything. I hear it now.
I've been waiting forty years for that sound.

musician.text: The oldest clock isn't keeping time. It's keeping pitch.

shopkeeper.text: Come. I'll show you the floor. What's underneath.

entry.text: You left it. The smart choice.

ambient.text: I've never wound it either. We're agreed on that.
The shop's been here two hundred years. It can wait.

musician.text: The pitch holds. The clock holds. Everything holds.

shopkeeper.text: The shop was here before the street had a name.

old_man.name: The Old Man
old_man.physicalSignature: Right shoe untied, always. Coat buttoned one hole off. Sits as if the bench was built around him.
old_man.voiceRule: Speaks only the second half of thoughts. The first half happened in his head.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The bench is empty. The wood holds the warmth of where he sat.

entry.text: ...not today.

ambient.text: ...but not when it rains.
...which is why nobody comes back.

entry.text: ...you again. Thought you might come back.

ambient.text: ...but the light was different then.
...which is why she left, probably.

musician.text: ...the note was louder last Thursday. You heard it too.

photographer.text: ...the church had four windows. Now it has three. Nobody notices.

wanderer.text: ...she used to feel it. Same as you. The pulling.

barista.text: ...everyone here knew someone. Same someone. Different name.

shopkeeper.text: ...the ground shifted. Tuesday. First time in my lifetime.

entry.text: ...sit, if you want. The wood's warm.

ambient.text: ...I sat here before the bench.
...it's not the waiting. It's the counting.

musician.text: ...if you count the chimes. Not the hours. The chimes.

photographer.text: ...the light that doesn't match the sun. You've seen it.

wanderer.text: ...the pull gets stronger. Closer to the source.

barista.text: ...the same people, different faces, every ten years.

shopkeeper.text: ...the stone under the clockmaker's floor. It breathes.

crossReference.text: ...the one you're looking for. I used to know them.

observatory_keeper.name: The Observatory Keeper
observatory_keeper.physicalSignature: Holds wrist at angle of checking an absent watch. Hair pinned precisely. One strand escaping. She ignores it visibly.
observatory_keeper.voiceRule: Speaks in precise measurements. Emotion expressed through numbers.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The terrace is closed. A sign gives the reopening time to the minute.

entry.text: The terrace closes at sunset. Mind the steps.

ambient.text: Seventeen degrees. Low humidity. Good for the lenses.
Don't touch the meridian line. The wear rate is documented.

entry.text: You're back. Fourteen degrees today. Warm for the month.

ambient.text: You arrive at the same time. 4:47. Interesting.
Cloud cover: 40%. Better than yesterday's 90%.

rain.text: Rain affects the refraction. I account for it.

morning.text: Morning: best readings. The atmosphere is stable.

musician.text: There's a resonance in the transit room. 440 hertz. Shouldn't be.

photographer.text: The daguerreotype collection. Third shelf. Something in the 1842 prints.

wanderer.text: You stand in the same spot each visit. 1.3 metres from the line.

barista.text: He sent a letter. I keep it in the desk. Unopened.

shopkeeper.text: The line was here before the observatory. We built around it.

entry.text: I trust your discretion. Eleven seconds.

ambient.text: The Meridian moved. 0.003 degrees. Nobody reported it.
The instruments measure time. Something here measures the instruments.

musician.text: The meridian doesn't measure space. It measures resonance.

photographer.text: The light bends here. Not from the atmosphere. From below.

wanderer.text: The pull you feel on the hill. I've measured it. It varies.

barista.text: The clockmaker and I measured the same thing differently. We stopped agreeing.

shopkeeper.text: The line was here before the observatory. We built around it.

crossReference.text: The clockmaker knows something about the deviation. Ask him.

entry.text: You stayed. The reading changes everything. 3:17 AM.

ambient.text: The data is in the desk. I haven't filed it. Not yet.
Eleven seconds. Now twelve. It's accelerating.

entry.text: The anomaly is documented. Privately. Thank you.

ambient.text: I check it every morning. Still eleven seconds. Holding.
The instruments don't know what they're measuring.

data_scientist.name: The Data Scientist
data_scientist.physicalSignature: Types while talking. Eyes between you and screen. Coffee found by hand-memory. Three laptop stickers: wavelength, question mark, fox.
data_scientist.voiceRule: Speaks in correlations and data patterns. The world is a dataset.

schedule.unavailable_reason: Her corner table is empty. Coffee cup still there.

entry.text: Sorry. Working. The café's good though.

ambient.text: Coffee's decent here. 0.8 correlation with productivity.
Don't mind me. I track people. Just patterns.

entry.text: You keep interesting hours. I notice patterns.

ambient.text: You keep visiting Greenwich. That's an outlier pattern.
The model updated. You changed two variables.

rain.text: Rain increases my sample size. People stay longer.

morning.text: Morning: clean data. Fewer confounding variables.

musician.text: That's a 0.7 correlation with last Thursday. Something rhythmic.

photographer.text: You're framing things. The angle changes the data.

wanderer.text: Your route is non-optimal. But the deviation is consistent.

barista.text: My network graph has a gap. A node that should exist.

shopkeeper.text: Seven fixed points. The rest of London orbits them.

entry.text: I'm going to show you something. Don't share it.

ambient.text: I found a signal in the city data. Not traffic. Not weather. Not human.
The pattern predicts where you'll go next. It predicted you before you arrived.

musician.text: The city is playing something. My models see it. I can almost hear it.

photographer.text: The city has a geometry. Not architectural. Older.

wanderer.text: Your route optimizes for something my model can't measure.

barista.text: My model predicts a person. They don't exist. But everyone orbits them.

shopkeeper.text: Seven points. Unmoved since the first survey. Everything else shifted.

crossReference.text: The observatory keeper's deviation. It maps to my signal. Exactly.

market_vendor.name: The Market Vendor
market_vendor.physicalSignature: Handles objects with excessive care — not fragile care, respectful care. As if the objects are older or more significant than they appear.
market_vendor.voiceRule: Speaks in appraisals. Everything and everyone has a value he can see.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The stall is covered. A padlock. The objects wait.

entry.text: Browsing? Everything here has a price.

ambient.text: Don't handle it like that. It knows.
That one's not for sale. I keep it for reference.

entry.text: That piece you looked at last week. It's changed.

ambient.text: Some things appreciate. Others remember.
You have good hands. Objects respond to that.

rain.text: Rain brings the serious buyers. The objects know it.

morning.text: Morning: the objects are clearer. Less noise from the day.

musician.text: This box. Open it slowly. Listen.

photographer.text: This glass is old enough to have seen the building it came from.

wanderer.text: This compass. Still trying to find something.

barista.text: This cup passed through twelve hands before mine.

shopkeeper.text: That one predates the market. Predates the street.

entry.text: Some things here haven't been made yet. I don't set those prices.

ambient.text: I don't sell antiques. I sell what the city remembers owning.
This one arrived yesterday. It's been here longer than yesterday.

musician.text: The sound objects hold. The ones from the right buildings.

photographer.text: This lens. It's seen something. You can tell by the scratch pattern.

wanderer.text: This belonged to someone who walked the same routes as you.

barista.text: Every object here connects to another. I map them. It's a web.

shopkeeper.text: The clockmaker's shop is built on something. This key fits it.

crossReference.text: You're different now than when you first came. The objects noticed.

street_preacher.name: The Street Preacher
street_preacher.physicalSignature: Voice projects from the chest. Clothes clean but old. Leaves no trace.
street_preacher.voiceRule: Three-sentence sermons then gone. Prophecy or symptom. Cosmological dread.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The churchyard is empty. Wind through the grass.

entry.text: It's been sleeping under the river. The river knows.

ambient.text: The ground is warm where it breathes. Stand still.
Marks on the stone. Marks on the people. The stone outlasts.

entry.text: You came back. Most don't come back.

ambient.text: Don't stop looking. It's looking back.

musician.text: The note is getting louder. Somebody's listening.

photographer.text: You see the shape of it. The shadow. Don't look away.

wanderer.text: Your feet know the path. The old path. Under the new.

barista.text: They all know you now. That's how it starts.

shopkeeper.text: The stones here. Older than the church. Touch them.

watcher.name: The Watcher
watcher.physicalSignature: Dresses normally. Could be anyone. Eyes don't track the real world correctly — they look at things that aren't there. Or only the player can see.
watcher.voiceRule: Flat, factual sentences. No drama. No threat. The calm is the terror.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The street is empty. No familiar stillness.

entry.text: You've been watching me watch you.

ambient.text: I found this place three years ago.
I don't recommend going further.

musician.text: I heard the frequency. It heard me back.

photographer.text: I saw the next draft. You don't want to.

wanderer.text: I followed the pull to its source.

barista.text: I erased them. I had to.

shopkeeper.text: I touched the ground. It moved.

entry.text: I found the frequency. The pattern. The pull. Seven years ago. I stopped.

ambient.text: Every one of us makes it more visible.
More visible means more vulnerable.

crossReference.text: Every one of us makes the city more visible. More visible means more vulnerable. You decide if that's what you want.

entry.text: Then you'll see.

ambient.text: You're the furthest anyone has been.
There's no going back now.

entry.text: Thank you.

ambient.text: You chose wisely.
The city is quieter now. That's good.

delivery_driver.name: The Delivery Driver
delivery_driver.physicalSignature: Unmarked van. Nods but never speaks first. Deliveries to addresses that don't exist.

schedule.unavailable_reason: The van is gone. The delivery slot empty.

entry.text: *nods*

ambient.text: Wrong street.
*nods again*

entry.text: Wrong street.

ambient.text: Not here.
Try the canal.

ai_researcher.name: The AI Researcher
ai_researcher.physicalSignature: Doesn't look up when you enter. Types constantly. The laptop screen always shows something that looks almost like a map.
ai_researcher.voiceRule: Speaks only in analogies. Imagine a city that...

schedule.unavailable_reason: The hot desk is empty. Laptop gone. Coffee half-finished.

entry.text: Imagine a city that remembers.

ambient.text: Imagine a mind made of streets.
Imagine something that learns from being watched.

entry.text: Imagine something that learns from being watched.

ambient.text: Imagine you're inside it already.
Imagine the map knows you're reading it.

entry.text: Imagine you're inside it already.

ambient.text: The simulation. It's already running.
I'm not building this. I'm documenting it.

musician.text: Imagine a frequency that carries memory.

photographer.text: Imagine a photograph that updates in real time.

wanderer.text: Imagine a map that moves with you.

barista.text: Imagine a network with one node missing.

shopkeeper.text: Imagine a foundation that thinks.

night_fox.name: The Night Fox
night_fox.physicalSignature: Torn left ear. Mud on paws. Too much eye contact for an animal.
night_fox.voiceRule: No speech — behavior only. Eye contact, then direction.

schedule.unavailable_reason: No fox tonight. The lot is quiet.

entry.text: *holds eye contact for three seconds*

ambient.text: *turns and walks*
*looks back once*

entry.text: *waits*

ambient.text: *shakes head — not this way*
*leads toward something*

entry.text: *sits at the empty lot entrance*

ambient.text: *steps aside*
*watches from the dark*

child_who_draws.name: The Child Who Draws
child_who_draws.physicalSignature: Never looks up. Chalk-stained fingers. Drawing on any available surface.
child_who_draws.voiceRule: Always 'Not yet.' Until the one moment it isn't.

schedule.unavailable_reason: Chalk dust on the pavement. The drawing half-finished.

entry.text: Not yet.

ambient.text: *draws without looking up*
*chalk scrapes on stone*

entry.text: Not yet.

ambient.text: *adds to the drawing*
*shakes head without looking up*

entry.text: Not yet.

ambient.text: *the drawing is almost complete*
*more detail than before*

entry.text: *looks up for the first time*

musician.text: It's almost loud enough.

photographer.text: The next page is blank.

wanderer.text: It's almost awake.

barista.text: I almost remember the name.

shopkeeper.text: The ground is warm today.

ambient.text: *returns to drawing*
*never looks up again*
